problems.diagnostic: /CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 107: Couldn't resolve reference to ConnectionEnd 'attitude_window'.
problems.cause: AGREE annex references ports (sensored_attitude, reference_torque) that are not connected via TCP to ACF as implied by R1; connection Cnx1 exists but AGREE clauses incorrectly map sensored_attitude to reference_torque, which are not the connected endpoints.
problems.minimal_edit: Remove the assume and guarantee clauses that incorrectly equate sensored_attitude with reference_torque and sun_direction with reference_force, since these ports are not connected by Cnx1/Cnx2; Cnx1 connects TCP.nominal_attitude to ACF.nominal_attitude, but these are not visible in AOCSprocessing type scope.

problems.diagnostic: /CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 108: Couldn't resolve reference to ConnectionEnd 'check_thresholds'.
problems.cause: ConnectionEnds 'attitude_window' and 'check_thresholds' do not exist as features of AOCSprocessing; validator correctly flags them as unresolved, and AGREE annex should not reference non-existent or implementation-subcomponent ports.
problems.minimal_edit: Delete all assume/guarantee clauses in the AGREE annex, as they reference non-existent or implementation-scoped ports (TCP.*, ACF.*) not visible at the component type level and misrepresent the actual signal flow.

executor_steps: Delete the entire annex agree {** ... **}; block under AOCSprocessing (lines 69-88) because it references non-visible subcomponent ports (TCP.nominal_attitude, ACF.nominal_attitude, etc.) and misuses component ports (sensored_attitude, reference_torque) as if they are connected by Cnx1/Cnx2, which they are not.

No replacement clauses are needed because the signal flow requirements R1 and R2 are structural and should be documented via AADL connections (Cnx1, Cnx2), not AGREE contracts, since AGREE cannot express subcomponent port connectivity at the component type level without visible variables.
